intent:
An empty seat in the classroom. The teacher writes the narrator's name on a paper at the front of the room (attendance/graded work — left vague to suit either). The narrator notices that the friend who normally sits behind them is absent today, and that the friend's name is not on the paper at the front. In the closer, the narrator writes the friend's name themselves — a quiet act of inclusion for a friend who isn't there to do it.

This is the second attempt at story 4 (the first, 私の名前, shipped 2026-04-29 21:14 and was reverted because it was a worksheet of bolt-on sentences with a teleporting friend and a mechanical pencil-transfer closer). The new §E.7 hostile prompt was written in response, then this story was drafted to pass it: the friend's absence (not their teleport-presence) is the dramatic axis, the pencil is what enables the closing act (not decoration), and the closer is a small kindness rather than a mechanical demonstration.

PREMISE CONTRACT (story 4)
  one_sentence_event:    The narrator, alone in the classroom because their friend is absent today, writes their friend's missing name on the front paper themselves.
  change_of_state:       at s0 the front paper has the narrator's name only and the friend's name is conspicuously absent. at the closer, the narrator has added the friend's name to the paper. the paper changes from incomplete to complete — completed by the wrong hand, but completed.
  scene_novelty_claim:   I am NOT writing a {home_morning, kitchen, walk_to_shop} story because the last 3 were. Scene is classroom — first appearance in the v2 corpus.
  anchor_novelty_claim:  Anchor is 鉛筆 (pencil). Last 5 anchors were tea_cup, apple, egg — pencil is fresh and the FIRST tool-anchor in v2 (1-3 were food/drink).
  closer_shape_claim:    Closer shape: noun-WA-noun-NO-noun-WO-verb-MASHITA (私は友達の名前を書きました). Action verb 書く in past-completed form. NOT noun-WA-noun (story 3), NOT noun-WA-noun-NO-noun-NI-noun (story 2), NOT noun-WA-noun-WO-noun (story 1). The past-completed form lands the act of inclusion as a fait accompli — the friend's name HAS been written by the narrator's own hand, in defiance of the official front-of-room paper that omits it.
  why_not_filler:        私 — narrates, holds the pencil (s2), performs the closing act of inclusion (s5). 先生 — performs the original act of name-writing (s1) that is asymmetric (only my name) — without this asymmetry there is no inclusion to perform. 友達 — defined by absence (s3, s4) — the absent friend IS the subject of the story; the friend cannot have an entrance because their not-being-here is the point. 鉛筆 — the means by which inclusion happens (s2 holding, s5 writing) — without it, the closer is impossible. Each role is structurally load-bearing.
  obligations_absorbed:  G013_mashita_past — s1 書きました (the teacher's writing of my name) AND s5 書きました (the narrator's closing act of writing the friend's name). The past-tense rhyme between s1 and s5 is structural: the teacher wrote some-but-not-all names; the narrator completes the omission. Both verbs are the same lemma (書く) which would mint twice under text_to_story's surface-keyed dedup if past and nonpast forms were used; using both as past avoids the duplicate-mint bug AND strengthens the s1↔s5 mirror. G007_te_form — s2 持って (the te-form connector inside te-iru). G035_arimasen — s3 前に友達の名前はありません (the friend's name's absence is the discovery beat that motivates s5; not bolted on). G008_te_iru intro — s2 持っています (continuous state of holding the pencil, the means-state that enables the closing act). G036_masen intro — s4 学校にいません (the friend's not-being-at-school today; the predicate that makes the absence a real condition, not a mistake). Every obligation lands on a sentence with a narrative reason to exist.

FORBIDDEN THIS STORY:
  scene_class    (last 3 stories): home_morning, kitchen, walk_to_shop
  anchor_object  (last 5 stories): apple, egg, tea_cup
  closer_shape   (last 3 stories): noun-WA-noun, noun-WA-noun-NO-noun-NI-noun, noun-WA-noun-WO-noun
  opening_seqs   (last 3 stories' first 3 content tokens):
    - 朝 / 家 / 台所
    - 朝 / 母 / 私
    - 朝 / 台所 / 卵

All four zones honored: scene_class=classroom, anchor=鉛筆, closer shape is noun-WA-noun-NO-noun-WO-verb (action verb, not on banned list), opening starts 私/は/学校 (no 朝).

Deferral note: seed_plan vocab is {学校, 先生, 鉛筆, 名前, 書く, 前, 後ろ} — all 7 used. Plus 友達 (the third character, required by characters_min=3). Total 8 mints, exactly at cap. The seed grammar (te_imasu, masen, ni_target) is satisfied: te_imasu via s2 持っています, masen via s4 いません. ni_target collapses to the existing G004_ni_location tag in the tagger and lands in s4 (学校に). No override consumed — all swaps are within the seed neighborhood.

NATURALNESS REWRITE 2026-04-30: s3 swapped vague 前に → concrete 紙に (前に friend's name isn't 'at the front' was ambiguous; 紙に friend's name isn't 'on the paper' is concrete and physical); s4 dropped 後ろの descriptor (positional friend descriptor + absent friend was awkward; 友達は学校にいません is cleaner); closer added 鉛筆で to reinforce G017_de_means (intro'd in story 3). Net: 紙 added as new mint, 前/後ろ dropped from corpus.

sentences.jp: 先生は私の名前を書きました。
私は鉛筆を持っています。
紙に友達の名前はありません。
sentences.en: My friend's name isn't on the paper.

sentences.jp: 友達は学校にいません。
私は鉛筆で友達の名前を書きました。
sentences.en: I wrote my friend's name with a pencil.
